title: Robot acquisition & RaaS guides
metaDescription: Buy vs lease vs RaaS for business robots: pricing models, contract terms, and when subscription robotics beats capital purchase across warehouse, cleaning, and restaurant categories.
intro: Acquisition model often matters as much as robot type. These guides explain RaaS, leasing, and buying — with category-specific pricing context and links to the matcher acquisition recommendation.

summarySections.heading: What this topic covers
bullets: RaaS vs buy economics over 3–5 years
When OpEx pilots de-risk first deployments
How PickTheRobot scores acquisition fit from budget preference

faqs.question: Is RaaS more expensive than buying robots?
faqs.answer: Usually yes over a 3–5 year horizon — often 30–50% more in total — but RaaS lowers upfront risk and bundles maintenance. It fits unproven utilization or changing layouts.

faqs.question: Can I switch from RaaS to purchase later?
faqs.answer: Many vendors offer buyout options or apply part of subscription fees toward purchase. Confirm terms before signing.
